element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player chooses from licensed MLB teams and rosters
Accurately recreated real MLB stadiums with distinct visual characteristics
Player adapts to unique viewpoint for pitch recognition and hitting
Player controls pitch selection and placement
Player times swings and adjusts approach based on pitch type
Players recognize and strategize around actual MLB player statistics and abilities

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based baseball rhythm with natural breaks between pitches and innings; tension builds through at-bat progression toward ball/strike outcomes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial/introductory difficulty with basic team matchups
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progression through season play against varying opponent skill levels
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship rounds with increased opponent competency
notable_spikes: Playoff/World Series matchups with stronger opponents

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard 9-inning baseball game with turn-based pitcher vs. batter interactions
Series of consecutive games building toward playoff qualification and championship
Each accurately-rendered MLB stadium presents distinct visual and dimensional characteristics affecting gameplay strategy

sources_used: wikipedia: World Series Baseball (1994 video game)
